title: Software Engineer III, Cloud Infrastructure
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience.
3 years of experience programming in Python, Go, Java, or C++.
3 years of experience with distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, or backend development.
Experience with Linux/Unix systems administration and troubleshooting.
preferred_qualifications: Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science or related technical field.
5 years of experience with data structures, algorithms, and software design.
Experience with container orchestration systems (Kubernetes, Mesos, or similar).
Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, Pulumi, or CloudFormation).
Experience with performance analysis, large-scale systems debugging, and capacity planning.
Contributions to open-source projects in the cloud-native ecosystem.
about_the_job:
Google Cloud's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, and infrastructure automation.

As a Software Engineer on the Cloud Infrastructure team, you will work on building and maintaining the foundational services that power Google Cloud Platform. You'll design, develop, and operate highly available distributed systems that serve millions of requests per second. The infrastructure you build will directly impact Google Cloud customers ranging from startups to Fortune 500 companies.

The Cloud Infrastructure team is responsible for the core systems that make GCP reliable, secure, and performant. This includes container orchestration (GKE), compute infrastructure, networking fabric, and the control plane services that tie everything together.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is $141,000-$202,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.
responsibilities: Write product or system development code for highly scalable cloud infrastructure services.
Review code developed by other engineers and provide feedback to ensure best practices (e.g., style guidelines, checking code in, accuracy, testability, and efficiency).
Contribute to existing documentation or educational content and adapt content based on product/program updates and user feedback.
Triage product or system issues and debug/track/resolve by analyzing the sources of issues and the impact on hardware, network, or service operations and quality.
Participate in, or lead design reviews with peers and stakeholders to decide amongst available technologies.
Participate in on-call rotations and incident response, conducting thorough post-mortems and implementing preventive measures.
benefits: Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance
Generous PTO and parental leave (18-24 weeks)
On-site fitness centers, meals, and wellness programs
Annual performance bonus and equity refresh grants

title: Software Development Engineer II, AWS Security
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related technical field.
4+ years of professional software development experience.
3+ years of experience with at least one modern programming language (Java, Python, Go, or C++).
Experience designing and building secure, highly available distributed systems.
Strong understanding of computer science fundamentals including data structures and algorithms.
preferred_qualifications: Master's degree in Computer Science, Security, or related field.
Experience with AWS services (EC2, S3, IAM, KMS, Lambda).
Professional security certifications (CISSP, CEH, or AWS Security Specialty).
Experience with cryptographic protocols and implementations.
Experience with security compliance frameworks (SOC2, FedRAMP, PCI-DSS).
Published security research or CVE discoveries.
about_the_job:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. AWS Security is at the heart of protecting the infrastructure that millions of customers trust with their most sensitive data and critical workloads.

As an SDE II on the AWS Security team, you will design and build security services that protect AWS infrastructure and our customers' data at unprecedented scale. You'll work on identity and access management (IAM), encryption services, threat detection, and security automation that operates across all AWS services and regions.

We are looking for engineers who are passionate about security, enjoy solving complex problems, and want to work on systems that protect some of the world's most critical infrastructure. You should be comfortable diving deep into ambiguous problems, have strong ownership mentality, and be excited about building secure systems from the ground up.

The base pay for this position ranges from $129,300 to $223,600 based on geographic market. This position is also eligible for sign-on bonus, relocation package, and annual performance bonus.
responsibilities: Design and implement security services that protect AWS infrastructure serving millions of customers worldwide.
Build and maintain identity and access management (IAM) systems including authentication, authorization, and credential management.
Develop encryption services and key management solutions using HSMs and cryptographic hardware.
Create automated security testing frameworks, vulnerability scanning tools, and security compliance automation.
Respond to security incidents with urgency, conduct root cause analysis, and implement preventive measures.
Work with compliance and legal teams to meet regulatory requirements including SOC2, FedRAMP, and GDPR.
benefits: Medical, dental, and vision coverage starting day one
Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) vesting over 4 years

title: Senior Software Engineer, Cortex XSIAM
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field.
5+ years of software engineering experience with focus on backend systems.
Strong proficiency in Python or Java with experience in distributed systems.
Experience building and operating large-scale data processing pipelines.
Experience with message queuing systems (Kafka, RabbitMQ, or similar).
Experience with search and analytics platforms (Elasticsearch, Splunk, or similar).
preferred_qualifications: Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related field.
8+ years of software engineering experience.
Experience in cybersecurity, threat detection, or security operations.
Experience with machine learning systems in production environments.
Experience with stream processing frameworks (Spark Streaming, Flink, or Kafka Streams).
Knowledge of security frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK, NIST, CIS).
Track record of leading technical projects and mentoring engineers.
about_the_job:
At Palo Alto Networks, we're committed to being the cybersecurity partner of choice. We're building the future of security operations with Cortex XSIAM—the industry's first autonomous security operations platform that uses AI to transform how security teams detect, investigate, and respond to threats.

As a Senior Software Engineer on the Cortex XSIAM team, you will architect and build the core platform components that process billions of security events daily. You'll work on cutting-edge machine learning pipelines for threat detection, high-throughput data ingestion systems, and real-time analytics engines that enable security teams to respond to threats in seconds rather than hours.

This role requires deep technical expertise in building large-scale distributed systems, experience with streaming data architectures, and passion for solving complex security challenges. You'll collaborate with world-class security researchers, data scientists, and engineers to build products that protect organizations from sophisticated cyber threats.

Our compensation ranges from $152,000 to $247,500, depending on location. This position is eligible for bonus and equity.
responsibilities: Architect and develop core platform features for Cortex XSIAM, processing 10+ billion events per day.
Build high-throughput, low-latency data pipelines using Kafka, Spark, and custom streaming solutions.
Design and implement machine learning infrastructure for real-time threat detection and anomaly detection.
Lead technical design discussions, create architecture documents, and conduct design reviews.
Collaborate with security researchers to implement new detection algorithms and threat intelligence integrations.
Mentor engineers, establish coding standards, and drive engineering excellence across the team.

title: Machine Learning Engineer, Siri and Information Intelligence
minimum_qualifications: Master's degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related technical field.
3+ years of experience developing and deploying ML models in production.
Strong programming skills in Python with experience in PyTorch or TensorFlow.
Experience with NLP tasks such as text classification, NER, or sequence modeling.
Strong foundation in statistics, linear algebra, and optimization.
preferred_qualifications: PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, NLP, or related field.
5+ years of industry experience in machine learning.
Publications in top-tier ML/NLP venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ACL, EMNLP).
Experience with speech recognition or audio processing.
Experience with on-device ML deployment and model optimization.
Experience with large language models (LLMs) and prompt engineering.
Experience with Swift and Apple platforms (iOS, macOS).
about_the_job:
The Siri and Information Intelligence team is creating groundbreaking technology for artificial intelligence and machine learning. We're building features that help over a billion users get things done through natural, conversational interactions with their devices.

As a Machine Learning Engineer on the Siri team, you will develop and deploy state-of-the-art NLP models for intent classification, entity extraction, and dialogue management. You'll work on speech recognition systems that understand users in noisy environments, and build conversational AI that can handle complex, multi-turn interactions.

A key focus of this role is on-device machine learning—building models that are small and efficient enough to run directly on iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch, and Mac while maintaining accuracy and protecting user privacy. You'll work at the intersection of research and production, taking models from concept to deployment at Apple scale.

Pay for this position ranges from $143,100 to $264,200 based on qualifications and experience. Apple employees also receive equity, discretionary bonuses, and comprehensive benefits.
responsibilities: Develop and optimize NLP models for intent classification, named entity recognition, and semantic parsing.
Build and improve automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems for diverse accents and languages.
Design conversational AI systems for multi-turn dialogues and context understanding.
Create efficient on-device ML models using quantization, pruning, and neural architecture search.
Analyze user interaction data (with strict privacy protections) to identify model improvements.
Write production code in Python and Swift, working with Core ML and Apple's ML infrastructure.
benefits: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage

title: Software Engineer, Full Stack - Reality Labs
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience.
3+ years of full-stack software development experience.
Proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript and at least one backend language.
Experience with React or similar component-based frontend frameworks.
Experience with relational databases and API design.
preferred_qualifications: Master's degree in Computer Science or related field.
5+ years of software engineering experience.
Experience with GraphQL and Relay.
Experience with 3D graphics, WebGL, or game development.
Experience with VR/AR technologies or spatial computing.
Experience with mobile development (React Native, iOS, or Android).
Experience building real-time collaborative applications.
Track record of shipping products used by millions of users.
about_the_job:
Reality Labs at Meta is building the future of connection through virtual and augmented reality. We're creating immersive experiences that will transform how people work, play, and connect with each other.

As a Full Stack Software Engineer on the Reality Labs team, you will build web and mobile applications that power the metaverse ecosystem. You'll work on creator tools, social experiences, and platform services that enable millions of users to connect in virtual spaces. Your work will span the entire stack—from React frontends to GraphQL APIs to distributed backend services.

We're looking for engineers who are excited about building at the frontier of technology. You should be comfortable with ambiguity, eager to learn new technologies, and passionate about creating great user experiences. This role offers the opportunity to work on products that will shape how humanity interacts in the coming decades.

Compensation for this position ranges from $147,000 to $208,000 depending on location and experience. Total compensation includes base salary, annual bonus, and equity (RSUs).
responsibilities: Build full-stack applications for VR/AR platforms using React, GraphQL, and Node.js.
Develop responsive web experiences with React, TypeScript, and modern CSS.
Design and implement GraphQL APIs serving millions of requests per day.
Create cross-platform mobile experiences using React Native.
Implement real-time collaboration features using WebSockets and custom protocols.
Optimize application performance for global scale and low-latency interactions.

title: Software Engineer, New Grad - Azure
minimum_qualifications: Currently pursuing or recently completed Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field.
Programming experience in one or more languages (C#, Java, Python, JavaScript, or C++).
Understanding of data structures and algorithms.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to learn quickly.
Graduation date between September 2025 and August 2026.
preferred_qualifications: Internship experience in software development.
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP).
Contributions to open-source projects.
Experience with web development (frontend or backend).
Familiarity with agile development methodologies.
Strong communication and collaboration skills.
about_the_job:
Microsoft Azure is the world's second-largest cloud platform, serving millions of customers from startups to Fortune 500 companies. As a New Grad Software Engineer, you'll join one of our Azure teams and work on services that power the digital transformation of businesses worldwide.

This is an incredible opportunity to start your career working on cloud infrastructure at massive scale. You'll learn from experienced engineers, receive mentorship, and work on projects that have real impact from day one. New grads at Microsoft work on the same challenging problems as senior engineers—we believe in giving ownership and responsibility early.

Our New Grad program includes structured onboarding, technical training, and a cohort experience to help you build connections across the company. You'll have opportunities to explore different areas of Azure and find the team and technology that excites you most.

Base salary ranges from $102,600 to $136,000 depending on location. Total compensation includes signing bonus, stock awards, and annual performance bonus.
responsibilities: Design and implement features for Azure cloud services used by millions of customers.
Write clean, maintainable code and participate in code reviews.
Learn and apply cloud computing concepts, distributed systems, and software engineering best practices.
Collaborate with team members to solve technical challenges and deliver high-quality software.
Participate in on-call rotations after ramp-up period (typically 6+ months).
Contribute to team documentation and knowledge sharing.

title: Senior Software Engineer, Content Delivery
minimum_qualifications: 7+ years of software engineering experience.
Strong proficiency in Java or a similar object-oriented language.
Experience building and operating large-scale distributed systems.
Deep understanding of networking concepts (TCP/IP, HTTP, CDN).
Experience with cloud infrastructure (preferably AWS).
preferred_qualifications: Experience with video streaming technologies and protocols (HLS, DASH).
Experience with content delivery networks (CDN) at scale.
Experience with edge computing and global traffic management.
Knowledge of video encoding and transcoding pipelines.
Experience with chaos engineering and building resilient systems.
Publications or talks at engineering conferences.
about_the_job:
Netflix is the world's leading streaming entertainment service with over 230 million paid memberships in more than 190 countries. Our Content Delivery team is responsible for streaming billions of hours of content to members worldwide, ensuring the best possible viewing experience.

As a Senior Software Engineer on the Content Delivery team, you will work on the systems that deliver video from our global Open Connect CDN to member devices. You'll optimize streaming quality, build tools for capacity planning, and develop algorithms that ensure smooth playback even under challenging network conditions.

This role requires deep technical expertise and the ability to work autonomously. At Netflix, we value freedom and responsibility—you'll have significant ownership over your projects and the authority to make important technical decisions. We're looking for engineers who thrive in ambiguous environments and are passionate about building systems at scale.

Netflix offers a unique compensation structure with a single top-of-market salary (no separate bonus or stock). Annual compensation ranges from $300,000 to $700,000.
responsibilities: Design and build systems that deliver petabytes of video content daily across 190+ countries.
Optimize streaming quality algorithms that adapt to network conditions in real-time.
Build and maintain tools for CDN capacity planning and traffic engineering.
Implement chaos engineering practices to ensure system resilience.
Lead technical design discussions and mentor other engineers.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including encoding, client, and infrastructure.
benefits: Unlimited PTO (with cultural expectation of ~4 weeks)
High degree of autonomy and ownership

title: Software Engineer, Payments Infrastructure
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
2+ years of software engineering experience.
Strong programming skills in at least one language (Ruby, Java, Go, or similar).
Understanding of distributed systems concepts.
Experience with relational databases and SQL.
preferred_qualifications: Experience in fintech or payments industry.
Experience building financial systems with strict consistency requirements.
Knowledge of payment processing (card networks, ACH, wire transfers).
Experience with event-driven architectures.
Strong attention to detail and quality.
Interest in helping businesses grow through financial infrastructure.
about_the_job:
Stripe builds economic infrastructure for the internet. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities.

As a Software Engineer on the Payments Infrastructure team, you will work on the core systems that process hundreds of billions of dollars annually. You'll build reliable, scalable infrastructure that handles payments across 135+ currencies and dozens of payment methods. The systems you build will directly impact the revenue of millions of businesses.

Payments infrastructure is uniquely challenging—every transaction must be processed correctly, and downtime directly impacts our users' revenue. You'll work on problems like exactly-once payment processing, distributed transaction management, and real-time fraud detection. This is a role for engineers who care deeply about correctness and reliability.

Compensation ranges from $140,000 to $199,000 depending on experience and location.
responsibilities: Build and scale payment processing systems handling millions of transactions daily.
Design distributed systems with strong consistency guarantees for financial operations.
Implement integrations with global payment networks and banking systems.
Improve system reliability, reducing latency and increasing availability.
Participate in on-call rotation to ensure 24/7 payment processing.
Work with product and compliance teams to launch new payment features.

title: Software Engineer I, Rider Experience
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent.
0-2 years of software engineering experience (internships count).
Programming experience in Swift, Kotlin, or React Native.
Understanding of mobile app architecture patterns (MVC, MVVM).
Strong problem-solving skills.
preferred_qualifications: Published mobile apps on App Store or Google Play.
Experience with iOS or Android SDK.
Familiarity with RESTful APIs and mobile networking.
Experience with mobile testing frameworks.
Interest in location-based services and maps.
Understanding of mobile performance optimization.
about_the_job:
Uber's mission is to reimagine the way the world moves for the better. Our Rider Experience team builds the features that millions of riders use every day—from requesting rides to tracking their driver to managing payments.

As a Software Engineer I on the Rider Experience team, you'll work on one of the most downloaded apps in the world. You'll build features that directly impact the experience of millions of daily active users across 70+ countries. This is a great opportunity for early-career engineers to learn mobile development at scale.

You'll be mentored by senior engineers and have access to Uber's extensive learning resources. We believe in giving meaningful work to engineers at all levels—you won't just be fixing bugs, you'll be building features that users see and love.

Base salary ranges from $106,000 to $141,000 depending on location. Total compensation includes annual bonus and equity.
responsibilities: Develop features for the Uber rider app used by millions of users daily.
Write clean, testable code in Swift or Kotlin.
Collaborate with designers and product managers to create great user experiences.
Learn mobile development best practices from experienced engineers.
Participate in code reviews and contribute to team documentation.
Debug and fix issues reported by users and internal teams.

title: Software Engineer, Search & Discovery
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
3+ years of software engineering experience.
Experience with Java, Kotlin, or similar backend languages.
Experience with search technologies (Elasticsearch, Solr) or recommendation systems.
Strong understanding of data structures and algorithms.
preferred_qualifications: Experience with machine learning for search ranking or recommendations.
Experience with A/B testing and experimentation frameworks.
Knowledge of information retrieval concepts.
Experience with distributed systems at scale.
Frontend experience with React or similar frameworks.
Passion for travel and community.
about_the_job:
Airbnb is a community built on connection—the connection between guests and hosts, travelers and locals, people and places. Our Search & Discovery team is responsible for helping guests find their perfect stay among millions of listings worldwide.

As a Software Engineer on the Search & Discovery team, you will build the systems that power Airbnb's search experience. You'll work on search ranking algorithms, personalization models, and discovery features that help guests find unique stays they'll love. Your work will directly impact booking conversion and guest satisfaction.

This role combines backend engineering with data-driven optimization. You'll work closely with data scientists and ML engineers to implement and iterate on ranking models, and you'll build the infrastructure that serves millions of search queries daily.

Compensation ranges from $154,000 to $200,000 base salary plus equity and bonus.
responsibilities: Build and optimize search ranking algorithms serving millions of queries daily.
Implement personalization features that tailor results to individual users.
Design and run A/B experiments to improve search quality metrics.
Build backend services in Java/Kotlin that power the search experience.
Collaborate with ML engineers to deploy and iterate on ranking models.
Contribute to search infrastructure improvements for scale and reliability.

title: Backend Engineer, Personalization
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
2+ years of backend development experience.
Proficiency in Java, Scala, or Python.
Experience with microservices architecture.
Familiarity with cloud platforms (GCP preferred).
preferred_qualifications: Experience with recommendation systems or ML infrastructure.
Experience with real-time data processing (Kafka, Spark Streaming).
Knowledge of collaborative filtering or content-based recommendations.
Experience with A/B testing at scale.
Passion for music and audio content.
Experience with Kubernetes and containerization.
about_the_job:
Spotify is the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with 600+ million users. Our Personalization team builds the recommendation systems that power Discover Weekly, Release Radar, Daily Mix, and other personalized experiences.

As a Backend Engineer on the Personalization team, you will build the infrastructure that delivers billions of personalized recommendations daily. You'll work on real-time ML serving systems, feature pipelines, and the backend services that connect our recommendation models to the Spotify experience.

This is a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of engineering and machine learning, building systems that help 600 million users discover music they'll love. You'll collaborate with ML researchers, data scientists, and product teams to bring new recommendation features to life.

Salary ranges from $131,000 to $187,000 depending on experience and location.
responsibilities: Build backend services that serve personalized recommendations to 600M+ users.
Design and implement feature pipelines for ML models.
Create real-time systems for serving and updating user preferences.
Optimize recommendation serving for low latency and high throughput.
Collaborate with ML teams to deploy and monitor recommendation models.
Participate in squad rituals and contribute to team culture.

title: Deep Learning Engineer, Autonomous Vehicles
minimum_qualifications: Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related field.
5+ years of experience in deep learning.
Strong proficiency in Python and deep learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow).
Experience with computer vision for perception tasks.
Experience with GPU programming and optimization.
preferred_qualifications: Experience with autonomous vehicle perception systems.
Experience with 3D object detection, segmentation, or tracking.
Publications in top-tier venues (CVPR, ICCV, NeurIPS, ICML).
Experience with CUDA and TensorRT optimization.
Experience with sensor fusion (camera, lidar, radar).
Knowledge of real-time systems and embedded deployment.
about_the_job:
NVIDIA is the pioneer of GPU-accelerated computing. Our DRIVE platform powers the autonomous vehicle systems of major automakers worldwide, from advanced driver assistance to fully self-driving capabilities.

As a Deep Learning Engineer on the Autonomous Vehicles team, you will develop perception models that enable vehicles to understand their environment. You'll work on 3D object detection, semantic segmentation, lane detection, and other computer vision tasks critical for safe autonomous driving.

This role combines cutting-edge research with real-world deployment. You'll develop models that run on NVIDIA's DRIVE AGX platform in production vehicles. Safety is paramount—your work directly impacts the safety of passengers and road users.

Compensation ranges from $180,000 to $339,250 base salary plus equity and bonus.
responsibilities: Develop deep learning models for autonomous vehicle perception (detection, segmentation, tracking).
Optimize models for real-time inference on NVIDIA DRIVE AGX hardware.
Design and implement training pipelines for large-scale perception datasets.
Collaborate with sensor fusion teams to integrate camera, lidar, and radar data.
Publish research and represent NVIDIA at academic conferences.
Mentor junior engineers and contribute to team technical direction.

title: Associate Software Engineer, Einstein AI
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or related field.
0-2 years of software engineering experience.
Programming experience in Python and/or Java.
Coursework or project experience in machine learning.
Strong foundation in mathematics (linear algebra, statistics, calculus).
preferred_qualifications: Master's degree in ML, AI, or related field.
Experience with deep learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow).
Experience with NLP or computer vision projects.
Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Salesforce).
Contributions to open-source ML projects.
Strong communication skills.
about_the_job:
Salesforce Einstein brings AI to the world's #1 CRM platform, helping businesses make smarter decisions with predictions, recommendations, and automation. Over 150,000 companies use Salesforce to grow their business, and Einstein AI is at the heart of our product innovation.

As an Associate Software Engineer on the Einstein AI team, you'll work on machine learning systems that power predictions and recommendations across Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, and more. This is an excellent opportunity for early-career engineers to learn ML engineering in a supportive, mentorship-focused environment.

You'll be part of our Futureforce New Grad program, which includes structured onboarding, executive mentorship, and a cohort of new grads to grow alongside. We believe in giving meaningful projects to engineers from day one.

Base salary ranges from $96,500 to $132,600 plus equity and bonus.
responsibilities: Develop and improve ML models for Einstein AI products.
Build data pipelines and feature engineering systems.
Write clean, well-tested code in Python and Java.
Learn ML engineering best practices from experienced mentors.
Participate in ML research reading groups and learning sessions.
Collaborate with product teams to understand customer needs.

title: Software Engineer, Data Engineering
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or related field.
3+ years of software engineering experience.
Strong programming skills in Scala, Java, or Python.
Experience with distributed systems and big data technologies.
Experience with Apache Spark or similar frameworks.
preferred_qualifications: Experience building data platforms or lakehouse architectures.
Contributions to Apache Spark or Delta Lake.
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Knowledge of query optimization and database internals.
Experience with streaming data processing.
PhD in databases, distributed systems, or related area.
about_the_job:
Databricks is pioneering the Data Lakehouse paradigm—combining the best features of data warehouses and data lakes. Founded by the original creators of Apache Spark, MLflow, and Delta Lake, we're building the unified platform for data and AI.

As a Software Engineer on the Data Engineering team, you will work on the core Databricks platform that processes exabytes of data for thousands of organizations. You'll build features for Delta Lake, optimize Spark query performance, and develop tools that help data engineers build reliable data pipelines.

This is an opportunity to work on industry-defining technology used by companies like Shell, Comcast, and the US Department of Defense. You'll collaborate with some of the best systems engineers in the world and contribute to widely-used open-source projects.

Compensation ranges from $153,000 to $215,000 base salary plus equity.
responsibilities: Build and optimize core features of the Databricks platform.
Develop features for Delta Lake and Databricks SQL.
Optimize Spark query execution and data processing performance.
Design systems for reliable, scalable data pipelines.
Contribute to open-source projects (Spark, Delta Lake, MLflow).
Collaborate with customers to understand and solve data challenges.

title: Systems Engineer, Edge Network
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
3+ years of systems engineering experience.
Strong programming skills in Go, Rust, or C.
Experience with Linux systems programming and networking.
Understanding of TCP/IP, HTTP, DNS, and BGP.
preferred_qualifications: Experience building CDN or edge computing systems.
Experience with performance optimization at scale.
Knowledge of web security (DDoS mitigation, WAF).
Experience with eBPF or kernel development.
Contributions to networking-related open-source projects.
Experience operating systems in a 24/7 environment.
about_the_job:
Cloudflare's mission is to help build a better Internet. Our global network spans 300+ cities and serves millions of web properties, from personal blogs to Fortune 500 companies. We block billions of cyber threats daily and help make the Internet faster and more reliable.

As a Systems Engineer on the Edge Network team, you will build the software that runs on Cloudflare's global edge. You'll work on our caching layer, load balancers, and the systems that terminate billions of HTTP connections daily. Your code will run in data centers on every continent.

This role requires deep systems knowledge and a passion for performance. You'll work on problems like optimizing TLS handshakes at scale, building efficient caching systems, and developing DDoS mitigation techniques. If you love understanding how computers really work, this is the role for you.

Salary ranges from $137,000 to $167,000 depending on experience.
responsibilities: Build and optimize edge computing systems serving billions of requests daily.
Develop caching, load balancing, and traffic management features.
Implement DDoS mitigation and security features at network scale.
Profile and optimize system performance for latency and throughput.
Debug complex distributed systems issues across global infrastructure.
Participate in on-call rotation supporting critical Internet infrastructure.

title: Software Engineer, New Grad - Feed
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or related field.
Graduating between December 2025 and August 2026.
Programming experience in Java, Python, or similar languages.
Understanding of data structures, algorithms, and software design.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
preferred_qualifications: Internship experience in software development.
Experience with distributed systems or backend development.
Familiarity with machine learning concepts.
Experience with databases (SQL and NoSQL).
Strong communication and teamwork skills.
Passion for LinkedIn's mission to connect professionals.
about_the_job:
LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network with 1 billion members. Our Feed team builds the personalized content experience that helps members stay informed and engaged with their professional network.

As a New Grad Software Engineer on the Feed team, you'll work on systems that serve billions of feed items daily. You'll build features that help members discover relevant content, learn from industry experts, and engage with their professional community.

LinkedIn's New Grad program (called REACH) provides structured mentorship, technical training, and a supportive cohort experience. You'll work on meaningful projects from day one and have access to learning resources across Microsoft.

Base salary ranges from $101,000 to $154,000. Total compensation includes signing bonus, stock, and annual bonus.
responsibilities: Build backend services that power LinkedIn's feed experience.
Write clean, tested code in Java following LinkedIn engineering practices.
Learn distributed systems concepts and apply them to real-world problems.
Collaborate with ML teams to implement feed ranking features.
Participate in code reviews and learn from experienced engineers.
Contribute to team on-call rotation after ramp-up period.

title: Software Engineer, Query Optimization
minimum_qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or related field.
5+ years of software engineering experience.
Strong programming skills in Java, C++, or similar languages.
Experience with database systems and query processing.
Deep understanding of algorithms and data structures.
preferred_qualifications: PhD in databases or related area.
Experience with query optimization or database internals.
Publications in database conferences (SIGMOD, VLDB, ICDE).
Experience with distributed query processing.
Knowledge of cost-based optimization and statistics.
Experience with cloud data warehouses.
about_the_job:
Snowflake delivers the Data Cloud—a global network where thousands of organizations mobilize data with near-unlimited scale, concurrency, and performance. We're revolutionizing the data warehousing industry with our cloud-native architecture.

As a Software Engineer on the Query Optimization team, you will work on the query compiler and optimizer that makes Snowflake the fastest cloud data warehouse. You'll develop optimization techniques for complex SQL queries, implement new query processing features, and ensure efficient execution across our distributed compute infrastructure.

This role is for engineers who love diving deep into systems. You'll work on compiler techniques, query planning algorithms, and distributed execution strategies. Your work will directly impact the performance of queries running across thousands of customers.

As a Software Engineer on the Frontend Platform team, you will build the infrastructure that powers Figma's web application. You'll work on our component library, performance optimization, and the foundational systems that enable our product engineers to build features quickly.

Figma's engineering challenges are unique—we push the boundaries of what's possible in a browser. You'll work with WebGL, manage complex state in real-time, and optimize performance for design files with thousands of elements. If you love frontend engineering at its most challenging, this role is for you.

Compensation ranges from $149,000 to $350,000 depending on experience.

Optimize web application performance for complex design documents.

As a Software Engineer on the Platform Security team, you will build security infrastructure that protects Coinbase's systems and customer assets. You'll work on authentication systems, access controls, security monitoring, and the tools that help engineering teams build secure software.

Security at Coinbase is a unique challenge. We're a high-value target for sophisticated attackers, and we secure assets that can't be reversed if stolen. You'll work on defense-in-depth strategies, implement zero-trust architecture, and build systems that detect and prevent attacks in real-time.

Compensation ranges from $147,900 to $174,000 base salary plus equity and bonus.

Develop security monitoring and anomaly detection systems.

You'll work on multiplayer editing, presence indicators, comments, and the systems that keep everyone in sync. When two people edit the same page, your code makes it feel seamless.

Real-time collaboration is one of the hardest problems in software engineering. You'll work with CRDTs, handle network partitions, and optimize for the latency of keystrokes. This role is for engineers who love building systems that feel instant and delightful.

Salary ranges from $130,000 to $200,000 depending on experience.

You'll analyze massive datasets of market data, develop statistical arbitrage strategies, and backtest trading signals using historical data.

This role combines cutting-edge quantitative research with real-world trading impact. Your models will directly influence trading decisions worth millions of dollars daily. This role offers the opportunity to work with sophisticated investment strategies across equities, fixed income, alternatives, and multi-asset funds. You'll collaborate with portfolio managers, traders, and clients to ensure risk is understood and managed effectively.

As a Consultant, you'll join case teams tackling strategic challenges for Fortune 500 clients. You'll conduct research and analysis, develop recommendations, and present findings to senior executives. You'll work closely with experienced consultants who will mentor you as you develop your skills.

Bain's apprenticeship model means you'll learn by doing, with increasing responsibility as you demonstrate capability. After two years, top performers are promoted to Senior Consultant with opportunities to pursue MBA sponsorship.

Starting base salary is $115,000 plus signing bonus and performance bonus.
responsibilities: Conduct research and analysis to support client recommendations.
Build financial models and perform quantitative analysis.

Develop presentation materials for client deliverables.
Collaborate with team members to solve complex problems.
Contribute to Bain's intellectual capital and best practices.

As a Deep Learning Solutions Architect, you'll work directly with enterprise customers to implement AI solutions using NVIDIA's technology stack. You'll optimize deep learning workloads, design inference architectures, and ensure customers extract maximum value from our platforms.

As a Financial Advisor, you'll work with clients to understand their goals and develop comprehensive financial plans. minimum_qualifications: Master's degree in Aerospace Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field.
3+ years of experience in spacecraft or complex system development.
Experience with systems engineering processes and tools.
Strong analytical skills with MATLAB, Python, or similar tools.

Experience with NASA missions or flight projects.

about_the_job:
NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory is a federally funded research and development center that has led humanity's exploration of the solar system. From Mars rovers to missions to Jupiter and Saturn, JPL builds robotic spacecraft that push the boundaries of what's possible.

As an Aerospace Systems Engineer, you'll contribute to the design and development of spacecraft systems for planetary exploration missions. You'll work on systems engineering activities including requirements development, interface control, integration, and test.

This is an opportunity to work on missions that inspire the world and advance our understanding of the universe. You'll collaborate with scientists and engineers at the forefront of space exploration.

Salary ranges from $100,000-$150,000 depending on experience.
responsibilities: Lead systems engineering activities for spacecraft development.
Develop system requirements and allocate to subsystems.
Manage interfaces between spacecraft subsystems.
Support integration and test activities.
Conduct trade studies and system analyses.
Contribute to proposal development for new missions.

As a Brand Manager, you'll own the strategy and P&L for your brand or product line. You'll make decisions that impact products on store shelves around the world.

The Allen Institute for AI (AI2) is a non-profit research institute founded by Paul Allen to conduct high-impact AI research for the common good. Our NLP team focuses on building AI systems that can understand, reason about, and generate natural language.

As a Research Scientist, you'll lead research projects exploring the frontiers of language understanding. Our work spans from fundamental research on language models to applied work on scientific document understanding and reasoning. We publish our research openly and release models and datasets to benefit the research community.

AI2 offers a unique environment combining the freedom of academic research with the resources and impact of industry. You'll have access to significant compute resources and collaborate with top researchers in a mission-driven organization.

Your work will directly impact how we evaluate companies and make investment decisions.

As a Clinical Data Scientist, you'll analyze clinical trial data to support drug development programs. You'll work with clinical teams to design analyses, develop statistical models, and generate insights that inform regulatory submissions and medical decisions.

This role offers the opportunity to apply data science to work that directly impacts patient health. You'll contribute to bringing new therapies to patients while advancing the science of clinical development.

This role offers unique technical challenges and meaningful impact. You'll work on problems at the frontier of AI/ML research with direct applications to national security. APL provides a research-oriented environment with opportunities for publication and professional development.

title: Economist, Financial Stability
minimum_qualifications: PhD in Economics or Finance.
Research experience in financial stability or banking.

Proficiency in Stata, R, or Python.
Excellent written and oral communication skills.
preferred_qualifications: Publication record in academic journals.

about_the_job:
The Federal Reserve Board is the central bank of the United States, responsible for monetary policy, financial regulation, and maintaining financial stability. The Division of Financial Stability conducts research and analysis to identify and monitor risks to the US financial system.

As an Economist, you will conduct original research on financial stability topics and contribute to policy analysis supporting the Federal Reserve's macroprudential responsibilities. Your work will inform decisions by the Federal Reserve Board and FOMC.

This position offers unique access to policymakers and data for impactful research. You will work alongside leading economists and have opportunities to publish and present at academic conferences.

Salary ranges from ,000-,000 depending on experience.
responsibilities: Conduct research on financial stability and systemic risk.
Analyze financial markets and institutions for emerging risks.
Support stress testing and scenario analysis for large banks.
Brief senior Federal Reserve officials on financial stability issues.
